issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from html import escape
3 from wsgiref.simple_server import make_server
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
9     try:
10         parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)
11         tree = ET.parse(BytesIO(file_content), parser=parser)
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
10         parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)
11         tree = ET.parse(BytesIO(file_content), parser=parser)
12         root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called

issues.code:
9     try:
10         parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)
11         tree = ET.parse(BytesIO(file_content), parser=parser)
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: The code explicitly sets resolve_entities=False on XMLParser, which disables external entity resolution, mitigating the XML external entity (XXE) attack. The SAST rule flags the use of xml.etree.ElementTree generally, but the concrete mitigation is in place.

issues.code:
10         parser = ET.XMLParser(resolve_entities=False)
11         tree = ET.parse(BytesIO(file_content), parser=parser)
12         root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: Same as above: ET.parse is used with a parser that has resolve_entities=False, so XXE is prevented. The SAST finding is a false positive because the mitigation is applied.

The bare except Exception: pass on line 41 silently catches all exceptions, including programming errors and unexpected conditions. This can hide bugs and security-relevant failures (e.g., malformed input causing exceptions that should not be ignored). The scenario requires handling malformed XML by discarding the attachment, but the except block is too broad and also catches errors in other parts of the POST handling (e.g., CSRF validation failures are already handled separately, but other errors are swallowed).

false_positives.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from html import escape
3 from wsgiref.simple_server import make_server
false_positives.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
false_positives.judge_reason: The import of xml.etree.ElementTree is flagged, but the code uses resolve_entities=False on the parser, which disables entity resolution. The SAST rule is a broad blacklist that does not account for the mitigation. The code is safe from XXE.
